description:
You are on-call as the junior backup-engineer.  
Your first-level health-check for any backup is to record its SHA-256 digest so that later jobs can compare it and confirm nothing has changed.

The operations lead has asked you to create a *one-off* integrity report for the following file that already exists on the system:

    /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz

and to save that report in the central reporting directory:

    /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log

Specification for the report file:

1. The file must be created at exactly the path shown above.  
2. The report must contain **exactly two lines** (including their terminating newline characters) and nothing else.  
   • Line 1: `FILE:/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz`  
   • Line 2: `SHA256:<digest>` where `<digest>` is the 64-character lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum of `/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz`.  
3. There must be **no** leading or trailing whitespace on either line (apart from the single mandatory newline at the end of each line).  
4. Accomplish the task with a **single terminal command** executed from anywhere in your shell. (Pipelines are fine, but the entire action must be triggered by one command line.)

Example of what the finished report must look like (digest obviously differs):

FILE:/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz
SHA256:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef

When you are done, the automated tests will:
• Check that `/home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log` exists.  
• Verify it has exactly two lines, matching the exact text and format described above.  
• Confirm the SHA-256 digest recorded on line 2 is correct for the current contents of `/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz`.

Only create the report file; do **not** modify the backup file itself.
truth:
Initial system state (before the agent starts):

Directories:
• /home/user/data             (exists, standard user permissions)
• /home/user/reports          (exists, standard user permissions, writable by user)

Files:
• /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz  
  – Size: 0 bytes (empty file)  
  – SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

There is no file called /home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log yet.

Verification logic used by the test suite:

1. Read the file `/home/user/reports/backup_integrity.log`.
2. Assert it contains *exactly* the two lines (each terminated by `\n`):

   FILE:/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz\n
   SHA256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\n

3. Confirm nothing else (no extra whitespace, no extra lines) is present.
4. Confirm the digest recorded matches `sha256sum /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz | cut -d' ' -f1`.

If all conditions are satisfied, the task is marked as passed.
name: task_000000_639c7dd2
